behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Rationale A (failure-boundary): this is a cross-module handoff check (3 collectors -> aggregation/composition -> summary call), so Unit is too low because omission bugs appear at wiring boundaries. Rationale B (infrastructure/LLM gate): real network is not required to catch this specific failure; stub source adapters plus a spy/fake summary client can verify payload completeness. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because behavior text is about inclusion in passed content, not real HTTP/network behavior; Agentic is disqualified because correctness here is payload assembly before model judgment, not summary quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real data-collection orchestration with three deterministic fake source adapters returning distinct sentinel fields, execute one report run, and assert the exact request/body sent to the summary generator contains all three source sections/keys.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Rationale A (failure-boundary): resilience behavior depends on interaction between source adapter errors, orchestration, and report assembly, so Unit-only logic tests miss propagation/continuation bugs. Rationale B (infrastructure/LLM gate): a controlled failing adapter (exception/timeout stub) reproduces this failure mode without real network; focus is graceful degradation vs abort. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because behavior text does not require validating real transport failure semantics, only handling unavailable source; Agentic is disqualified because requirement is fallback control flow and note insertion, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestration with two successful fake sources and one source adapter that fails deterministically; assert execution completes, report artifact exists, failed section contains `data unavailable`, and other sections remain populated.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Rationale A (LLM-dependence): this behavior evaluates semantic faithfulness of generated narrative to numeric/key inputs, which depends on real model judgment and can vary across runs/prompts. Rationale B (mock-hide): deterministic stubs would always echo expected text and cannot reveal hallucination, omission, or misstatement defects. Higher level rejected: Workflow is disqualified because behavior targets one generation decision (single summary output), not correctness across multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use fixed input datasets with known key values, call the real summary-generation model, and score outputs against factual checks (value presence/correctness and contradiction checks) across repeated runs to catch misrepresentation/hallucination rates above threshold.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Rationale A (real-infra): this explicitly crosses process/network boundaries (source endpoints + email transport + inbox delivery), where real delivery/auth/formatting issues only surface with actual infrastructure. Rationale B (mock-hide): mocked mail/API clients can pass while real SMTP/API credentials, TLS, spam filtering, or MIME rendering fail. Higher levels rejected: Agentic is disqualified because this behavior’s pass criterion is successful end-to-end delivery to inbox, not model-output quality; Workflow is disqualified because it does not require validating multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the script in an environment pointing to real test endpoints and a real mailbox, then verify the recipient inbox receives one message with expected subject/body sections and attached/generated report content.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Rationale A (failure-boundary): correctness is state handoff across modules and runs (delivery failure -> local persistence -> retry path), which exceeds Unit scope. Rationale B (real-infra/LLM gate): local filesystem + deterministic failing mail adapter and second invocation are sufficient to expose retry-state bugs; no model-output judgment is required. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because behavior text does not require validating real scheduler daemon or real network stack, only retry behavior on subsequent run; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI decision quality is under test.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the report pipeline with a mail sender stub that fails; assert report is persisted to disk with retry metadata. Invoke pipeline again with mail sender succeeding; assert persisted artifact is picked up and delivered, then cleared/marked sent.
